source_text: Administrators must be able to disable OCR, translation, and condition assessment when needed. Paid enrichments must be explicitly enabled, traceable, and cost-controlled.
ai_draft: Skrbniki morajo moči OCR, prevajanje in ocenjevanje stanja po potrebi tudi izklopiti. Plačljive obogatitve morajo biti izrecne, sledljive in stroškovno nadzorovane.
corrected_text: Skrbniki morajo imeti možnost, da po potrebi izklopijo OCR, prevajanje in ocenjevanje stanja. Plačljive obogatitve morajo biti izrecno omogočene, sledljive in stroškovno nadzorovane.

example_scorecard.scorecard_note: The draft preserves most of the intended meaning, but it is not publication-ready because grammar, modality, and policy phrasing require native-speaker correction.

issues.root_cause_hypothesis: literal translation of an English modal construction
issues.reviewer_rationale: The AI draft is ungrammatical in Slovenian. The corrected version uses the natural modal construction for policy language.

issues.root_cause_hypothesis: awkward source-language word order carried into the target language
issues.reviewer_rationale: The corrected wording is more natural and removes unnecessary emphasis that does not fit the sentence context.

issues.root_cause_hypothesis: dictionary-level word choice without enough product-policy context
issues.reviewer_rationale: The requirement concerns explicit enablement of paid enrichment, not a descriptive adjective applied to the enrichments themselves.

overall_lesson: Even when the overall meaning is preserved, AI-generated translations may require native-speaker review to correct subtle issues in grammar, modality, terminology, and domain-specific register. These issues are often difficult to detect through automated quality metrics alone.

recommended_uses.benefit: Shows reviewers how to document subtle but high-impact corrections that automated QA may miss.
Provides a realistic benchmark case for modality, register, and terminology failures in production-style text.
Supplies a concise before-and-after example of acceptable policy and governance phrasing.
Demonstrates why review records should capture reasoning and likely failure mode, not only the final correction.
Supports the argument that human review remains essential even when AI preserves the broad meaning.
